mnemonics: ACCURATE - A mnemonic for data quality dimensions: Accurate, Complete, Consistent, Unique, Reasonable, Accessible, Timely, and Explainable.
keyPoints: Data Quality ensures data is fit for its intended use in operations, decision making, and planning.
Poor data quality leads to inaccurate analytics and poor business decisions, costing time and money.
Data Quality Management is an ongoing process involving profiling, cleansing, monitoring, and prevention.
commonMistakes: Assuming data is clean at the source and loading it without quality checks into the warehouse.
Focusing only on technical metrics (e.g., null counts) and ignoring business context and user requirements.
Treating data quality as a one-time ETL project task instead of a continuous, business-owned process.
